senses.definition: A female royal family member who is the daughter of a king or queen, or the wife of a prince.
examples: The princess wore a sparkling gown to the royal ball.
Every year, the princess visited the town to meet her people.

senses.definition: The realm or state of being a princess; the status or position of a princess.
examples: Historically, princessdom carried many ceremonial duties.
She wondered about life in princessdom and its expectations.

senses.definition: The French word for princess; used in English texts to refer to a princess in a French context or title.
examples: Marie, princesse de Bretagne, greeted the guests.
The manuscript mentions the princess and her attendants.

senses.definition: Plural of princess; more than one female royal family member who is a princess or wife of a prince.
examples: The princesses attended the charity event together.
Two princesses spoke at the ceremony.

senses.definition: Having qualities associated with a princess, such as grace, beauty, or noble bearing.
examples: She moved with princesslike grace.
The banquet featured princesslike gowns.

senses.definition: Characteristic of a princess; curiously used to describe manner or behavior expected of a princess.
examples: She spoke in a princessly tone.
He opened the door with a princessly chivalry.

senses.definition: A prestigious private university in New Jersey, USA; known for programs in science, engineering, and humanities.
examples: He studied at Princeton University.
Princeton researchers published a breakthrough paper.

senses.definition: A place name or fictional location; not a standard common noun in contemporary English.
examples: They wandered through Princewood forest in the story.
Princewood is described as a quiet, ancient grove.

senses.definition: A misspelling or variant of 'Principia'; typically refers to Isaac Newton's work 'Philosophiæ Naturalis Principia Mathematica' or a similar title.
examples: He studied excerpts from Principia.
The Principia laid foundations for classical mechanics.

senses.definition: To make something resemble or adopt the style or status of a prince or princely; to regalize.
examples: The ceremony was princified with gold and velvet.
They princified the hall for the gala.

senses.definition: To make something princely or noble in appearance or style.
examples: They plan to princify the hall for the royal visit.
The decorations are meant to princify the event.

senses.definition: Present participle of princ e? Likely a misspelling or nonstandard form; no standard sense in contemporary English.
examples: The text contains princing errors.

senses.definition: The most important person or thing; the head of a school; a sum of money not including interest.
examples: The principal spoke at the assembly.
The principal amount of the loan is $10,000.

senses.definition: First or most important; main.
examples: Her principal reason for leaving was health.
The principal features of the plan were outlined.

senses.definition: Regions ruled by princes; a territory or domain of a prince.
examples: The principalities in the region were historically small and independent.
Two principalities formed an alliance.

senses.definition: The territory ruled by a prince; a prince's realm.
examples: Andorra is a principality.
The principality enjoyed a degree of autonomy.

senses.definition: Mainly; for the most part.
examples: The project is principally funded by volunteers.
She spoke principally about climate change.

senses.definition: The quality or state of being principal; chiefly the importance or prominence.
examples: The principalness of his idea was clear from the outset.

senses.definition: Plural form of principalness; the state of being principal in multiple cases.
examples: The document lists several principalnesses of the proposal.

senses.definition: More than one principal; people in charge or heads of organizations; or the main points of a lesson.
examples: The principals of the school met with parents.
The lesson covers the principals of budgeting.

senses.definition: The position or role of a principal; the duties and responsibilities of being a school head.
examples: Her principalship lasted twenty years.
The principalship required strong management skills.

senses.definition: Multiple instances of the role or office of a principal.
examples: The district saw several principalships become vacant.

senses.definition: The office or period of a prince or ruler; a period of princely power.
examples: The prince's rule marked a new principate.
Historically, the principate shaped the region's politics.

senses.definition: Plural of principate; periods or offices of princes.
examples: The principates of several rulers overlapped in time.

senses.definition: The Italian and Spanish word for prince; used in names or titles in those languages.
examples: The article refers to Principe Island.
El principe de la historia aparece al principio.

senses.definition: The plural of the Italian/Spanish word for prince; used in proper names or foreign contexts.
examples: Los principes de la casa participaron en la ceremonia.
